title: phanera semibifida var. subglabra
intro: Phanera semibifida var. subglabra, also known as the lesser staff vine, is a climbing plant species belonging to the family Moraceae. Traditionally, it has been used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues like coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Phanera semibifida var. subglabra include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to make teas, tonics, and remedies to promote overall well-being.
